issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users across all interactive elements that do not define their own focus styles, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets an explicit min-height of 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. While this meets the minimum 24px requirement, the vertical padding of 0.625rem (7.5px) is less than 8px and does not provide sufficient compensation to reach 44px total height.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for buttons when navigated via keyboard, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size and above the 24px minimum. No padding compensation is provided to increase the touch area to 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px with min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum).

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address element uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) text on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.2:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1442: 
  color: var(--colour-neutral-400);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p element uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) text on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.2:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1457: 
  color: var(--colour-neutral-400);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none and only an outline‑offset, providing no visible focus style (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard users lose the focus indicator on every interactive element, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) without adding any visible alternative, so buttons lose their focus ring for keyboard navigation, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 736: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has min-height: 36px and vertical padding of 6 px (from --space-1-5), giving a total height of 48 px but the padding is less than the recommended 8 px on each side, so the effective touch target may be perceived as too small for some users, breaching WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.fix: Line 1958: `.testimonial-card figcaption p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: Contact section description uses #aaaaaa against white background, failing WCAG AA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2027: `.contact-section > p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); text-align: center; max-width: 560px; margin-inline: auto; margin-bottom: var(--space-10); padding-inline: var(--space-6); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use #aaaaaa against white background, providing insufficient contrast for form assistance text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2056: `.input-hint { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer and account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The global removal of text underlines combined with insufficient non-colour differentiators in account navigation and footer navigation causes links to be identifiable only by colour, violating SC 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 893: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`
Line 2089: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus style. Keyboard users cannot see focus, violating WCAG 2.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: .btn:focus-visible removes the outline without adding a visible alternative, so button focus is invisible for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: .nav-toggle:focus-visible sets outline: none and only offset, leaving no visible focus cue for the mobile navigation toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button in the site search removes the outline with no replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to locate the button focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation lose their focus outline without any alternative, breaking focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has a min-height of 20px, which is smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum 24 px touch target, making it hard for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon defines a 20 px min-width and min-height, resulting in a touch area smaller than the required 24 px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which can cause words to overlap and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration, eliminating the visual cue that text is a link, and no other styling (color change, border) distinguishes links, violating SC 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 240: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines a transition that includes transform, causing motion, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion media query that disables or overrides this animation, affecting users who reduce motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 480: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }
Line 950: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes a transform transition for hover effects, but no reduced‑motion override is provided, which can cause unwanted motion for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 560: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }
Line 960: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has a transform transition for hover effects, yet no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disables it, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 620: .hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }
Line 970: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline width. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see a focus ring on any focused element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 342: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). The padding compensation (6px vertical) results in a total height of 32px, still below the 44px recommended size and the explicit min-height is under 24px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 656: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. With padding of 10px, the total size reaches 40px, but the explicit minimum dimensions are non-compliant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 667: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1261: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (line 599-604) and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active (line 615), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule overriding this transform animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: Add inside existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) or create new: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform (line 773) and transform effects on hover (line 778), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 783-788 is empty and does not disable the transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 783-788: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover, but this specific selector is not covered by the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 993-997 which only targets .pricing-card:hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 993-997: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 339: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on code elements
issuesFound.explanation: The code element uses color: var(--colour-brand-secondary) (#bb88ee) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text under WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 316: code { ... color: var(--colour-brand-secondary-hover); ... } (where #581c87 provides ~7:1 contrast) or adjust background to darker shade.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote elements
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against the page background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 322: blockquote { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... } (where #475569 provides ~5.6:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global a selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change) on hover or focus for all links by default. Many link instances (navigation, social links) rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 226: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR add a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; } to provide the non-colour differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight) to distinguish links from surrounding text. This forces users to rely solely on colour to identify links, violating WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector removes the default outline (`outline: none`) and only sets an offset, providing no visible focus indicator for any keyboard‑navigable element. Keyboard‑only users lose the ability to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible focus style, e.g.  
  Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` removes the outline (`outline: none`) without supplying any alternative visual cue, causing loss of focus indication for mouse‑initiated focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide a visible focus style, e.g.  
  Line 260: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img (transform transition)

issuesFound.fix:
Add an override inside the reduced‑motion block, e.g.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)

issuesFound.fix:
Add an override, e.g.  
  Line 620: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from knowing their input contains errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the start of the function before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function reveals the mobile navigation drawer and sets _open = true, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving blind users unaware that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu changes the visible content significantly but does not announce this state change via an aria-live region. Blind users are not notified when the dropdown appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` immediately before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function displays the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technology users cannot perceive that the dropdown is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu hides the panel from view but does not announce this change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not realize the dropdown has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');` immediately before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function sets item.open = true and reveals the panel, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer removes it from view but does not announce this state change. Users may not realize the menu has closed and that focus has moved.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the start of the function before the `_open = false;` line.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ answer hides the content without announcing the state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the answer has been collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.close(): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} — collapsed').replace('{title}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` at the start of the function before setting item.open = false.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-native buttons lack Enter/Space handlers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers may be rendered as non-button elements (divs/spans) with role="button" and tabindex="0", but the keyboard handler only manages Arrow keys, Home, and End. It does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the accordion, making it inaccessible to keyboard users who cannot use the mouse.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, leaving keyboard users stranded at the trigger button. When it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button, causing focus loss. The _trap variable is declared but never activated in the shown code.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open(): Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after `_lockScroll();`. Line mobileNav.close(): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before `_unlockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance in the dropdown menu controller adds the “open” class to the panel but never calls setAttribute(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') or updates trigger.ariaExpanded. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria‑expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The function _closeInstance removes the “open” class and hides the panel but never sets aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility state stale.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2240: add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); inside _closeInstance when the dropdown is closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller fires custom events ‘dropdown:open’ and ‘dropdown:close’ but does not write to any aria‑live region. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); within the same functions.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded to true on the trigger, and close does not set it to false. The accessibility state does not reflect the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3110: add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening, and in close add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.bind is used for the email <input>, but the validator only adds an error message element; it never sets aria‑invalid='true' when the field is invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3408: after inlineValidator.bind(emailInput, ['required','email']); add emailInput.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); and modify the validator to set 'true' on error (or use srUtils.markInvalid which also sets aria-invalid).

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> in the contact form is bound with inlineValidator but the validator does not set aria‑invalid when the email is invalid, so assistive technologies are not informed of the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3785: ensure the validator calls setAttr(emailField, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on error and removes it on success (or use srUtils.markInvalid / markValid).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu missing live‑region announcement also violates JS‑G (user action not announced)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open/close actions change visible content (show/hide menu) but no dedicated live‑region update is performed, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback about the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> to the page (or reuse an existing one) and write “Menu opened” / “Menu closed” after each toggle.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list opening is not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion panel is opened ( suggestionListRenderer.show ), no aria‑live announcement is made. Users navigating via screen readers are unaware that suggestions are now available.
issuesFound.fix: After suggestionListRenderer.show() add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchSuggestionsOpened || 'Suggestions available'); or set aria‑expanded on the input to true and announce the change.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list closing is not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion panel is hidden ( suggestionListRenderer.hide ), no live‑region update occurs, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that suggestions have been dismissed.
issuesFound.fix: After suggestionListRenderer.hide() add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchSuggestionsClosed || 'Suggestions hidden'); or set aria‑expanded to false and announce.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not set aria‑expanded (duplicate of Issue 5) – already covered.
Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑controls when drawer has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In register the code sets aria-controls only if drawer.id exists. If the drawer lacks an id, the trigger has no aria-controls, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every drawer element has a unique id (e.g., if (!panel.id) panel.id = shortId('dropdown-panel');) or always set aria-controls to that generated id.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function declares a _trap variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap when the navigation drawer opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer and access content behind the modal overlay, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form status not associated with input via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form creates a status element for announcements but does not associate it with the email input using aria-describedby. Screen reader users may not hear status updates when focused on the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3345: After creating _statusEl, add `if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('aria-describedby', _statusEl.id);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hover timer may timeout too quickly
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu uses a 150ms hover delay (CONFIG.navigation.hoverCloseDelay) before closing when mouse leaves. While not a strict violation, this may be too short for users with motor impairments who need more time to move to the submenu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: Increase the delay or make it configurable with a longer default: `inst.hoverTimer = setTimeout(() => { ... }, CONFIG.navigation.hoverCloseDelay || 300);` (change default from 150 to 300 or more).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons use aria-selected without proper role
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function sets aria-selected on the billing period buttons (month/quarter/year). However, these are native button elements without role="tab". The aria-selected attribute is only valid on roles option, tab, or gridcell. Using it on buttons creates invalid ARIA that assistive technologies may not handle correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2636: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive));` since these function as toggle buttons, or alternatively add `btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab');` before setting aria-selected.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav overlay not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav closes, the overlay is hidden using the hidden attribute, but during the open state, the overlay is made visible without ensuring it is properly exposed. However, more critically, when the drawer closes, focus should return to the trigger button. The close function does not return focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1884: In the close function, ensure focus is returned: `_trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` (add after _unlockScroll if not already present).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes and locks scrolling but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded state to true, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu the _openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria‑expanded to true on the trigger element, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The open() method in faqAccordion adds the open class and animates the panel, yet it never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute to true, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without aria‑hidden when closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function of mobileNav removes CSS classes and adds hidden to the drawer, but it never sets aria‑hidden="true". When the drawer is hidden via off‑screen CSS, AT may still expose it.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); in close() and remove it (or set to false) in open().

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() and _closeInstance() emit custom events but do not write to any element with role="status"/"alert" or aria‑live, so users are not informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); or update a dedicated live region.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not have aria‑hidden set to false when opened (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: While open() adds CSS classes to display the drawer, it never removes or sets aria‑hidden to false, so AT may still consider the drawer hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); or setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); when the drawer becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1926: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visible but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2376: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions lack live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes visible content but does not announce the state change via the live region. Screen-reader users receive no feedback that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2491 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before emit. Line 2509 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before emit.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2485: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function animates the FAQ panel open and sets internal state, but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4127: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (showing active/inactive state) but the code only toggles CSS classes. It never sets aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3387: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The code queries the navigation toggle button and sets aria-expanded and aria-controls, but never ensures the button has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML lacks text, the button will be announced only as "button".
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2360: After `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls') && _drawer.id) { ... }`, add:
  `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_trigger.textContent.trim()) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu);`

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button is queried and hidden via aria-hidden, but the code never sets an aria-label. When the button becomes visible, screen readers will announce it without a name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2635: After `_initialised = true;`, add:
  `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is given role="switch" and aria-checked, but no aria-label is applied. Users will not know what the switch controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3720: After setting role, add:
  `if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.switchToAnnual || 'Toggle annual billing');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the “open” CSS class to the drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the hamburger trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `open()` (or after the drawer becomes visible).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` reveals a dropdown panel and adds the open class, but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button; only the close path sets `aria-expanded="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but never updates `aria-expanded` on the question button (the close path does set it to “false”).
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email input lacks aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email field is validated with `inlineValidator.bind()`, which adds an error message but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input when the value is invalid. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the inline‑validation routine, after detecting an invalid value, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` (and remove it on valid input).

issuesFound.title: contact form “full_name” field missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The “full_name” input is validated via `inlineValidator.bind()` but the validation does not set `aria-invalid="true"` when the field fails the required/minLength checks.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation handler for this field, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: contact form “email” field missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 5 for the email input; validation adds an error message but does not set `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when the email is invalid.

issuesFound.title: contact form “subject” field missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input is validated but never receives `aria-invalid="true"` on failure.
issuesFound.fix: Set `aria-invalid` to true in the validation error path.

issuesFound.title: contact form “message” textarea missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation of the message field does not set `aria-invalid="true"` when the content is too short or too long.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` on validation error.

issuesFound.title: contact form “consent” checkbox missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The required consent checkbox is validated but the error state does not set `aria-invalid="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: When the checkbox is unchecked on submit, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus updates visibility but does not write a message to any `aria-live` region, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open() function after setting _open = true. Line 2952: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the close() function after setting _open = false.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing when a dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3040: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance() after setting inst.open = true. Line 3065: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _closeInstance() after setting inst.open = false.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid form fields. Conversely, markValid() does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3250: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside markInvalid(). Line 3260: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`) inside markValid().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the init() function after confirming _trigger exists.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function emits a custom event but does not announce the state change via the liveRegion announcer. Unlike the open() function which announces "Navigation menu opened", the close action is silent, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2952: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` inside the close() function before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module does not announce when dropdown panels open or close. Users navigating with screen readers receive no feedback when activating a dropdown trigger or when a dropdown closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3040: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` inside _openInstance(). Line 3065: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');` inside _closeInstance() (or in the close handler).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions update the aria-expanded attribute but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users do not receive confirmation that the answer has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4180: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title) || '${item.title} expanded');` inside open(). Line 4200: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title) || '${item.title} collapsed');` inside close().

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates the displayed prices and billing period UI but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the pricing display has switched between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` inside setPeriod() after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function in productFilter updates the visible count of products but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the current filter criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` inside _updateCountBadge() after updating the textContent.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _onChipClick() handler in activeFilterChips removes filter chips from the UI but does not announce the removal or the resulting filter state change. Screen reader users are not informed that a filter has been removed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3940: Add `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed. ${productFilter.getState().activeCategory === 'all' ? 'Showing all products' : 'Showing filtered products'}');` inside _onChipClick() after calling the filter update methods.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the modal overlay. Additionally, focus is not returned to the trigger button on close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });` inside init() after _drawer is confirmed. Line 2930: Add `_trap.activate();` inside open() after opening the drawer. Line 2952: Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called in close() (already present but will now work).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button does not update aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance` in the “Dropdown Menu Controller” section) adds/removes CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute on the panel, but never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded true on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In the FAQ accordion (`open(item)` in the “FAQ Accordion Controller” section) the panel is shown and animated, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"` (only set to `"false"` on close). This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` at the start of the `open(item)` function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation (`close()` in the “Mobile Navigation Toggle” module) hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and unlocking scroll, but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer element. When the drawer is hidden it remains in the accessibility tree, causing focus to land on hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` after removing the open classes, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove that attribute (`removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden')`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s `_closeInstance` function hides the panel by adding the `hidden` attribute after a transition, but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel. Some browsers keep the element focusable while hidden, which can confuse assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; ensure `openInstance` removes the attribute (`removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden')`).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function only links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen-reader users are not informed of the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3580: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` at the beginning of the `markInvalid()` function before the `addDescribedBy()` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button retains its previous aria-expanded state (or lacks the attribute), failing to communicate the collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the beginning of the `close()` function before the early-return check.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() omits aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes the error description but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid state announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3595: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`) at the beginning of the `markValid()` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu panel does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true", leaving screen-reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1683: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` inside the `_openInstance()` function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the updated result count and visible items are not announced to screen-reader users because the function updates the DOM without writing to an aria-live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2845: Inside `applyFilters()`, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu panel does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false", causing assistive technologies to continue announcing the menu as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `if (!inst.open) return;` inside the `_closeInstance()` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not announce the state change to screen-reader users, leaving them unaware whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3125: Inside `open()`, after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`, add:
  ```
  if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);
  ```
  Line 3145: Inside `close()`, after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add:
  ```
  if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ accordion item does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, so screen-reader users cannot identify which question is currently open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3125: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` inside the `open()` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer does not move keyboard focus into the drawer, leaving keyboard users stranded on the trigger button while the new content appears elsewhere.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() fails to return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer does not return focus to the trigger button that opened it, causing keyboard focus to be lost to the document body.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1560: At the end of the `close()` function (after `_unlockScroll()` and before the emit call), add:
  ```
  if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (monthly/annual) updates visible prices but does not announce the change or new prices to screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3325: Inside `setPeriod()`, after `_syncToggleState(period);`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: search clear button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (selected via SELECTORS.searchClear) may not have an accessible name if the HTML lacks text or an aria-label, and the JavaScript does not ensure one is present.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2445: After `_clearBtn = qs(cfg.clearSelector);`, add:
  ```
  if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) _clearBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search');
  ```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button toggles the panel visibility but never updates its aria‑expanded state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open/closed status.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger does not reference the navigation drawer via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship needed for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are given aria‑controls but are never given an initial aria‑expanded value, so screen readers cannot tell whether the answer is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator adds error messages but never sets aria‑invalid on the associated input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The badge that shows the number of matching products is updated visually but no aria‑live region announces the new count, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of filter results.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan price changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, plan prices are updated visually but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology cannot hear the new price.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips component does not announce changes
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing filter chips updates the UI but no aria‑live region informs screen‑reader users of the current active filters.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer hide animation does not clear aria‑hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the drawer element is hidden via CSS classes but aria‑hidden is never removed, leaving the hidden region still perceivable to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hide does not set aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden by adding a CSS class, but aria‑hidden is not applied, so screen readers may still read the hidden menu items.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid/markValid missing aria-invalid handling
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() and markValid() utility functions manage error messaging via aria-describedby but do not set or clear the aria-invalid attribute. Form validation errors are not communicated as invalid data to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1320 (in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line 1335 (in markValid, after removing aria-describedby): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to toggle aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for state management.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2020 (in _openInstance, after inst.open = true;): setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 2035 (in _closeInstance, after inst.open = false;): setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus updates the visual UI but does not announce state changes to screen readers. Users are not informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2025 (in _openInstance, after emit): liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 2045 (in _closeInstance, before emit): liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280 (in open function, after item.open = true;): setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content but provides no auditory feedback via live regions. Users may not realize the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2290 (in open function, after emit): liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);
  Line 2310 (in close function, after emit): liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (monthly/annual) updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users may miss the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550 (in setPeriod function, after _syncToggleState): liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the results to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750 (in applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge): liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not establish an aria-controls relationship between the trigger button and the drawer element. Screen readers cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1821 (in init function): setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads. The button starts in a collapsed state but lacks the attribute indicating this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820 (in init function, after _initialised = true;): setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens elsewhere.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1855 (in open function, after _lockScroll();): const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880 (in close function, at end): if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing focus move
issuesFound.explanation: Opening an FAQ item does not move focus to the answer panel or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users must manually navigate to the newly revealed content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295 (in open function, after item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');): const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(item.panel)[0] || item.panel; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: Closing an FAQ item does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2315 (in close function, at end): item.trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). The drawer cannot trap focus, allowing users to tab out of the modal context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1815 (in init function, after _initialised = true;): _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });
  Line 1856 (in open function, after _lockScroll();): if (_trap) _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The register function adds aria‑expanded="false" initially, but the _openInstance and _closeInstance functions that show/hide the panel never toggle aria‑expanded on the trigger. Screen‑reader users cannot know the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') or use toggleAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', true/false).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed, no liveRegion.announce call is made, so users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After calling _openInstance or _closeInstance, invoke liveRegion.announce with a message like "Menu opened" or "Menu closed".

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the panel but does not call liveRegion.announce or liveRegion.announceAssertive to inform users that the answer is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce (e.g., `${item.question} – answer expanded`) after setting item.open to true.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a FAQ item does not announce the collapse, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce (e.g., `${item.question} – answer collapsed`) after hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: Form fields validated with inlineValidator do not set aria‑invalid on error (JS‑D) – newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are shown visually, but inlineValidator only adds error text; it never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the <input>. Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate or after calling it, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when invalid and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Form fields validated with inlineValidator do not set aria‑invalid on error – contact form first‑name input
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 5; the first‑name field’s validation errors are not reflected via aria‑invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure setAttr(_firstNameEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on error and remove on success.

issuesFound.title: Form fields validated with inlineValidator do not set aria‑invalid on error – contact form email input
issuesFound.explanation: Email validation errors are not exposed to AT because aria‑invalid is never set.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when invalid.

issuesFound.title: Form fields validated with inlineValidator do not set aria‑invalid on error – contact form message textarea
issuesFound.explanation: Validation of the message field lacks aria‑invalid updates, preventing AT from detecting the error.
issuesFound.fix: Apply setAttr(_messageEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on error and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden via CSS classes and scroll‑lock, but aria‑hidden is never set to true when the drawer is closed, so the hidden navigation remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and remove it in mobileNav.open.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce the change via live region (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the UI updates prices but no liveRegion.announce call informs screen‑reader users of the new period.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce (e.g., `Pricing switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden (JS‑F) – panel hidden via CSS class only
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by removing the open class, but aria‑hidden is not set, leaving the hidden menu in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when closing and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when shown (JS‑F) – panel shown via CSS class only
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens, the panel becomes visible but aria‑hidden is not removed, which may cause duplicate content announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden') when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls set when drawer ID is missing (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: Without aria‑controls, assistive tech cannot associate the button with the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has an id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)) and always set aria‑controls on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items lack aria‑expanded announcement on state change (JS‑G) – missing live region for each toggle
issuesFound.explanation: Users are not informed when an FAQ answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce calls in both open and close functions with appropriate messages.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list does not set aria‑live on the container (JS‑C) – missing live region for dynamic suggestions
issuesFound.explanation: Updates to the suggestion list are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(listEl, 'aria-live', 'polite') or use a dedicated live region to announce “X suggestions available”.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function visually indicates validation errors by adding CSS classes and updating the error element, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the input field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4195: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, String(!result.valid));` to set aria-invalid to "true" when invalid and "false" when valid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the function updates the visible card set and count badge but does not announce the result count or filter change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technology users cannot perceive that the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside the function after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users, who may not perceive the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3395: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input element, leaving screen readers unaware that the listbox is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2940: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" to indicate the panel is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate the collapsed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2225: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the function after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = true, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to false, creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3808: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons cycle between states but the function only updates CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button and aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons, so screen reader users cannot identify which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3328: Add inside the forEach loop: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization function sets up the mobile navigation trigger button but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" to reflect the closed state on page load. Screen readers may not recognize it as a disclosure control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after verifying _trigger and _drawer exist.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion list, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load to indicate the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2995: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands an FAQ item but does not announce the state change via the live region, despite having an announceToSR parameter that is unused for this purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3818: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` before emitting the event.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function collapses an FAQ item but does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content is now hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3855: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` before emitting the event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not announce the availability of the menu or the number of items to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2212: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes content from view but does not announce that the menu has closed, potentially confusing screen reader users about the current focus context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2235: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users cannot immediately navigate the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: Add `_drawer.focus();` or `getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]?.focus();` after opening the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance adds the “open” CSS class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance removes the “open” class and hides the panel but does not reset aria‑expanded to false on the trigger, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); inside _closeInstance when the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open/close functions emit custom events but never write to a live‑region, so users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback that a menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the panel, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened) or liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed) respectively.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The element _toggleEl acts as a binary switch (monthly ↔ annual) but the code never toggles aria‑pressed, violating the ARIA button‑state pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In _onToggleClick (and in init after setting the initial state) add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', String(isYear)); where isYear reflects the current period.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates UI text but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users are not informed of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After setPeriod call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual) or liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingMonthly) accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button _trigger opens _drawer but never receives an aria‑controls reference, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After locating _trigger and _drawer, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); in mobileNav.init().

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav open/close actions are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: open() and close() toggle the navigation drawer but do not write to a live‑region, leaving AT users unaware of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of open() call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen); and at the end of close() call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer hidden via CSS class is not marked aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: close() removes the “open” class from _drawer without setting aria‑hidden="true", so the off‑canvas menu remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute (or set 'false').

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: _inlineValidator._renderResult adds visual error classes but never applies aria‑invalid="true" to the field, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: When result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when true, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); in _renderResult.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The same _renderResult function removes visual error styling but does not explicitly remove aria‑invalid, leaving the attribute stale on the element.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch (valid case) add field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); to ensure the error state is cleared.
