issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded when suggestions open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox with a suggestion listbox. When the suggestion panel opens and closes, the code updates internal state but does not toggle aria-expanded on the input element to communicate the listbox visibility to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves to the skip link, but there is no live region announcement informing screen reader users that the action completed successfully or that they are now at the top of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _onClick, after the setTimeout block): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the open function updates the visual state and animates the panel height, but unlike the close function which sets aria-expanded="false", it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to indicate the dropdown state. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch initialization does not set initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox is initialized without setting aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the suggestion listbox is initially collapsed. This is required for proper combobox behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init, after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes but do not programmatically communicate the pressed state to screen readers using aria-pressed. This is required for toggle buttons that remain pressed when activated.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tabs behave as a single-select button group where one button is always active, but they only update visual styling. They lack aria-pressed attributes to indicate which filter is currently active to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _syncTabAriaStates): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach loop immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the code associates the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. When validation passes, it does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _renderResult, valid branch): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` before calling `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`
  Line (in _renderResult, invalid branch): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` before calling `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization does not set initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. Screen readers may assume the menu is expanded by default or report an indeterminate state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init, after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu initialization does not set initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are registered during initialization, the trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" to reflect that the panels are initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in register, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved to the drawer container or first focusable element, trapping keyboard users in the background. When closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button. This violates WCAG focus management requirements for modal dialogs/drawers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open): Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus();` after opening the drawer
  Line (in close): Add `_trigger.focus();` after closing the drawer

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but screen reader users receive no notification that the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in setPeriod, after updating all plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the visual change is animated but no announcement is made to screen readers. The STRINGS.faqOpened and STRINGS.faqClosed messages exist in the configuration but are never invoked via the live region announcer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` after opening the item
  Line (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` after closing the item

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and a count badge is updated, but screen reader users are not informed how many products are now visible or that the filter has been applied. The emit event is insufficient for screen reader notification.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in applyFilters, inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the navigation state change, leaving them unaware that the menu is now available or that it has been dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, screen reader users receive no feedback about the state change. While focus management handles some cases, users who trigger the dropdown via click without moving focus into it need to know the panel appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the hamburger button has no aria‑expanded attribute, leaving its collapsed state ambiguous to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after obtaining _trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); so the initial state is announced.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given aria‑haspopup but never receive an initial aria‑expanded value, so their collapsed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register after creating inst add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); and toggle it in _openInstance / _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance and _closeInstance add/remove CSS classes and emit custom events, but they never set aria‑expanded on the button that controls the panel. Screen readers cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In _ openInstance add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); and in _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons receive aria‑controls and role="button" but are never given an initial aria‑expanded attribute, leaving their collapsed state undefined.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse after creating item set setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); and toggle it in faqAccordion.open/close.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close animate the panel and emit events but never modify aria‑expanded on the associated button, so assistive tech cannot detect the state change.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); and in close add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes the “is‑open” class to hide the drawer but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The element remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute (or set false).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hidden via CSS without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance removes the “open” class and later adds hidden attribute, but the panel may be hidden earlier by CSS only, leaving it focusable. No aria‑hidden is set at the moment of hiding.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance immediately setAttr(panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); and in _openInstance remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: User actions (mobile navigation open/close, dropdown toggle, FAQ toggle) are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The UI changes (menu opening, drawer opening, FAQ answer expansion) emit custom events but do not write a message to any element with role="status"/"alert" or aria‑live. Users of screen readers receive no audible feedback about the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After each successful open/close, call liveRegion.announce(…) with a concise message, e.g. liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened') or 'FAQ answer expanded'. Ensure a dedicated live‑region element exists (the app already creates one for other components).

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled open and closed via the open() and close() functions, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1478: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the start of the open() function.
  Line 1498: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the close() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no live-region announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the state change. The emit() calls only fire custom events for analytics, not for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1478: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` after setting _open = true.
  Line 1498: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` after setting _open = false.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance() and _closeInstance(), but neither function sets aria-expanded on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1623: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the start of _openInstance().
  Line 1639: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu updates the visual UI but does not announce the state change via the live region. Users relying on screen readers may not realize the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1623: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` in _openInstance().
  Line 1639: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` in _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the FAQ accordion adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", but the open state is never communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2448: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the start of the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function ensures aria-haspopup is set but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the dropdown is registered in a closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1683: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in register() after ensuring aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item changes the visible content but does not trigger a live-region announcement. Screen reader users may not be aware that the answer panel has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2448: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));` in open().
  Line 2475: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));` in close().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox opens and closes the suggestions panel via _open() and _close(), but the aria-expanded attribute on the input element is never updated to reflect the visibility of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2154: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in _open().
  Line 2163: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in _close().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch recent queries not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the search input is focused and empty, _showRecentQueries() displays the recent searches panel without announcing it via a live region. Screen readers remain silent while the visual content changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: Add `liveRegion.announce('Showing recent searches');` at the end of _showRecentQueries().

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product grid visibility and count badge but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2672: Add after the filter logic completes: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the price displays but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3034: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` in setPeriod().

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in markInvalid().

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1103: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`) in markValid().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch init() function sets up the combobox pattern but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the input element before the user interacts with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in init() after confirming _inputEl exists.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function stores a reference to a focus trap but never calls _trap.activate(), meaning keyboard focus is not constrained to the drawer when it opens, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1478: Add `_trap.activate();` in open() after setting _open = true.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The render() function updates the active filter chips UI when filters change, but does not announce the added or removed filters via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current filter state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: Add `if (chips.length) liveRegion.announce('Active filters: ' + chips.map(c => c.label).join(', '));` in render().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function does not set aria-controls on the trigger button to establish the programmatic relationship with the drawer element, which is required for the expanded/collapsed state to be meaningful.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: Add `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);` in init().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function (and the `open()` / `close()` helpers) add or remove CSS classes to show/hide the navigation drawer, but they never set or update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is not given an `aria-controls` attribute that references the drawer element (`_drawer`). Without this relationship assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After locating `_drawer`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'))`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed (`_closeInstance`), the code removes the `open` class but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the panel. The panel remains perceivable to screen readers even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance` after removing the class, add `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `_openInstance` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.toggle(item)` function calls `open(item)` / `close(item)`, which add/remove CSS classes but never set the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened or closed, the code emits custom events (`faq:open`, `faq:close`) but does not provide an audible announcement. Users of screen readers receive no feedback that the answer has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question))`; after closing, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question))`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter results update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the number of results. Users cannot know how many products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, set its text in an element with `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`)`).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed and aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The simple billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` and `tabindex`, but its pressed state is only reflected by a CSS class on the inner track; the element never receives `aria‑pressed="true|false"` or an accessible label indicating the current period.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick` after toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(pricing.getPeriod() === 'year'))` and update `aria-label` to reflect the active period.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑controls linking to the price table
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) change the displayed prices but do not have an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the price table or the region that updates, leaving assistive tech without a clear relationship.
issuesFound.fix: After parsing each button, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(priceTableEl, 'price-table'))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed (`close()`), the code removes the open class but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on `_drawer`. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute referencing its panel, breaking the ARIA disclosure relationship.
issuesFound.fix: When registering a dropdown (`register(trigger, panel)`), add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel'))`.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message element via aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but FormGroup is not provided with the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage component is never rendered with that ID. This creates a dangling reference that points to a non-existent element, preventing screen readers from announcing the error message.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the navigation submenu does not receive an aria-expanded attribute that reflects the isOpen state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ item button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses each FAQ item does not have an aria-expanded attribute reflecting the isOpen state, so assistive technologies cannot convey the disclosure state.

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing buttons indicate the selected period only via visual styling; they lack aria-pressed, preventing AT users from knowing the current selection.

issuesFound.title: Annual billing button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 4 for the annual billing option.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing aria-describedby target

issuesFound.title: TextInput component does not apply aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an “invalid” prop but never maps it to aria-invalid, so inputs never convey error state to AT.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter submit button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button receives a “loading” prop (which the Button component does not recognize) instead of the required “isLoading” prop, so aria‑busy is never set and AT cannot know the button is busy.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute on the underlying input element. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form fields using this component.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button that toggles the navigation submenu does not have an aria-expanded attribute to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup incorrect error prop causing missing error message
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed the prop `error` instead of `errorMessage`, and no `errorId` is provided. Consequently, the error message element is never rendered, creating a broken aria-describedby reference on the email input (pointing to 'newsletter-email-error' which does not exist).

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The submit Button has aria-describedby={statusId} referencing 'newsletter-status', but this element only exists when status is 'success' or 'error'. When the form is idle, the reference is broken.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: Remove aria-describedby={statusId} from the Button, or conditionally render it only when status !== 'idle': aria-describedby={status !== 'idle' ? statusId : undefined}

issuesFound.title: FAQItemComponent button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion button that shows or hides the FAQ answer lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen readers from knowing its expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput includes aria-describedby pointing to "newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id is ever rendered, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ErrorMessage with matching id or remove the aria-describedby. Example fix:
  Line 1150: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "newsletter-email-error" : undefined} />
  Also add after the TextInput:
  Line 1155: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The name TextInput sets aria-describedby to "contact-name-error", but no element with that id is rendered, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ErrorMessage with matching id or remove the aria-describedby. Example:
  Line 1300: <TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? "contact-name-error" : undefined} />
  Then add:
  Line 1305: <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput includes aria-describedby="contact-email-error" without a corresponding element, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ErrorMessage with matching id or remove the aria-describedby. Example:
  Line 1315: <TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "contact-email-error" : undefined} />
  Then add:
  Line 1320: <ErrorMessage id="contact-email-error" message={email.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The subject TextInput sets aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" but no element with that id exists, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ErrorMessage with matching id or remove the aria-describedby. Example:
  Line 1330: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? "contact-subject-error" : undefined} />
  Then add:
  Line 1335: <ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" message={subject.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The message Textarea includes aria-describedby referencing "contact-message-error", which is never rendered, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an ErrorMessage with matching id or remove the aria-describedby. Example:
  Line 1345: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={["contact-message-error", counterId].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} />
  Then add:
  Line 1350: <ErrorMessage id="contact-message-error" message={message.state.error} />

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby points to non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-describedby` references `newsletter-email-error`, but no element with that ID is rendered, causing a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Remove the `aria-describedby` prop or add an element with `id="newsletter-email-error"` that displays the error message.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button loading state not communicated (uses wrong prop)

issuesFound.fix: Line 970: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>…</Button>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The input’s aria-describedby points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id exists, so AT users receive no error description.
issuesFound.fix: Add an element with id="newsletter-email-error" (e.g., <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />) or change aria-describedby to reference the existing error element.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria-describedby references non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea’s aria-describedby includes “contact-message-error”, but no such element is rendered, so error information is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: Render an error element with id="contact-message-error" or adjust the aria-describedby list.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button toggles the answer panel but does not communicate its expanded state via aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell if the answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has an aria-describedby attribute pointing to "newsletter-email-error", but the ErrorMessage component is rendered without this ID because the errorId prop is not passed to FormGroup. This creates a broken reference that prevents error messages from being announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1930: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a navigation landmark but lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav: `<nav aria-label="Skip links">`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea includes "contact-message-error" in its aria-describedby, but the FormGroup is not provided with errorId="contact-message-error", so the reference is broken when errors appear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2075: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section element is a major page region but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute, making it difficult for screen reader users to navigate to this specific landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page navigation link is indicated visually via CSS class but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from identifying it as the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 930: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar is a distinct page section containing a heading, but the section element lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section: `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button is an IconButton containing only the "›" character without an aria-label, leaving screen reader users without context for the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755: Add aria-label="Next testimonial": `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references "contact-name-error" via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup is not provided with errorId="contact-name-error", so the error message lacks this ID when rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a label element that does not use the htmlFor prop to associate with the input, nor does it wrap the input. This breaks the programmatic association between the label and the form field, leaving the field without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 780: Add htmlFor={htmlFor} to the label: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and no visible text or aria-label, leaving screen reader users unaware of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: Add aria-label to the IconButton: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu uses a visual indicator class but does not expose aria-current="page" to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 980: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy, leaving users unaware that content is loading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 650: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation is a nav element without an accessible name. Since multiple nav elements exist (SkipLinks, DesktopNav, Footer), screen reader users cannot distinguish between them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: Add aria-label="Main" to the nav: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element containing a heading with an ID, but the article lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as its accessible name, making it hard to identify in card grids.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references "contact-email-error" via aria-describedby, but the errorId prop is missing from FormGroup, breaking the association with the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2055: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references "contact-subject-error" via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop to set this ID on the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick missing interactive semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot focus or activate this element to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" aria-label="Close navigation" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation panel is an IconButton without an aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 960: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu": `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button is an IconButton containing only the "‹" character without an aria-label, so screen reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial": `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself, which may cause screen readers to incorrectly identify the modal boundaries and focus scope.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Remove aria-modal from the overlay div and add it to the dialog div: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}><div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which prevents screen readers from treating it as a modal dialog and restricting navigation to within the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 940: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div: `<div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={\`\${SITE.name} navigation\`} ...>`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing descriptive aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The primary call-to-action link in each pricing plan card uses generic text (e.g., "Get started") without an aria-label that includes the plan name, making it indistinguishable from other plan CTAs when navigating by links list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: Add aria-label including plan name: `<a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={\`\${plan.ctaLabel} for \${plan.name} plan\`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md'].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a navigation dropdown does not expose its open/closed state to assistive technology because it lacks an aria‑expanded attribute. Keyboard and screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 306:    <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes the off‑canvas mobile navigation panel does not convey its expanded state (isOpen) via aria‑expanded, leaving AT users unaware of the panel’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560:    <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation submenu button missing aria-expanded (second submenu)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands the “Resources” submenu in the mobile nav lacks aria‑expanded, preventing AT from knowing its state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 608:    <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FAQ item button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Each FAQ accordion button toggles its answer panel but does not include aria‑expanded, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150:    <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons indicate the active filter only visually; they lack aria‑pressed, so AT cannot perceive which tab is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800:    <button type="button" className={...} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons do not expose their pressed state via aria‑pressed, leaving AT users unaware of the current billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950:    <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={...} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Slide divs that are not currently visible are not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to read content from all slides instead of just the active one.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1165: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton is rendered without the required aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without context for the button's purpose or current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~552: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~332: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~371: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The section element lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing its visible h1 heading, reducing navigability for screen reader users browsing by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~688: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element containing skip links lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks when multiple nav elements exist on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~242: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element lacks an aria-label, which is needed because there are multiple nav elements on the page (desktop, mobile, footer).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~498: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label, which is needed because there are multiple nav elements on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~552: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page has a visual style (mobile-nav__link--current) but lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~552: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself, which may cause assistive technology to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1554: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}><div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The clickable backdrop overlay is decorative but not marked with aria-hidden, potentially causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~552: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The group of trust badges lacks an aria-label, making it unclear what the group represents when encountered by screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~688: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges and certifications">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection CTAs group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The group containing the primary call-to-action buttons lacks an aria-label to identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~688: <div className="hero-section__ctas" aria-label="Call to action">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk character used as a required field marker is not hidden with aria-hidden, potentially causing screen readers to announce "star" before the "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~293: *

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a navigation submenu does not expose its open state to assistive technology, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the off‑canvas mobile menu does not convey its expanded state, leaving AT users unaware of the menu’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Hero video play/pause button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles background video playback does not expose its pressed state, preventing AT users from knowing whether the video is playing.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
The toggle buttons for billing period do not indicate which option is selected, so AT cannot convey the current selection.  
  Fix (monthly button):  
  Line 720:    <button  
  Line 721:     type="button"  
  Line 722:     id="billing-monthly"  
  Line 723:     aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}    /* added */  
  Line 724:     onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}  

  Fix (annual button):  
  Line 730:    <button  
  Line 731:     type="button"  
  Line 732:     id="billing-annual"  
  Line 733:     aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}    /* added */  
  Line 734:     onClick={() => onChange('annual')}

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation:
The filter tab buttons do not expose their pressed state, so AT users cannot tell which filter is active.  
  Fix (example button):  
  Line 660:    <button  
  Line 661:     type="button"  
  Line 662:     className={…}  
  Line 663:     aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}    /* added */  
  Line 664:     tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1}

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon‑only button that switches themes has no aria‑label, making it invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close IconButton missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is icon‑only and lacks an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” carousel control is an icon‑only button without an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The “next” carousel control is an icon‑only button without an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input missing associated label
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> is not programmatically associated with its visible label, so screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix:
Add htmlFor/label linkage or aria-label. Example using FormGroup’s htmlFor prop:  
  Line 540:    <FormGroup  
  Line 541:     label="Email address"  
  Line 542:     htmlFor="newsletter-email"    /* added */

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” textarea missing associated label
issuesFound.fix: add htmlFor="contact-message" to the surrounding FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm input aria‑describedby references non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: aria-describedby points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id exists, so AT cannot read the error.
issuesFound.fix:
Change the reference to the actual error element id or render an element with that id. Example:  
  Line 548:    aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-status' : undefined}    /* updated */

issuesFound.fix: Add aria-busy={isSubmitting}.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the <a> when item.current is true.
